description: Cluster is the Schema for the clusters API. A Cluster represents a Kubernetes cluster created and managed by Cluster API.

apiVersion.description: APIVersion defines the versioned schema of this representation of an object.

kind.description: Kind is a string value representing the REST resource this object represents.

name.description: Name must match the cluster name used in ROSAControlPlane and ROSACluster.

namespace.description: Namespace must match the namespace used in ROSAControlPlane and ROSACluster.

spec.description: ClusterSpec defines the desired state of a Cluster.

apiServerPort.description: The port on which the API server is listening.

pods.description: The network ranges from which Pod networks are allocated.

services.description: The network ranges from which service VIPs are allocated.

controlPlaneEndpoint.description: ControlPlaneEndpoint represents the endpoint used to communicate with the control plane.

host.description: The hostname on which the API server is serving.

port.description: The port on which the API server is serving.

controlPlaneRef.description: Reference to a ROSAControlPlane object that holds the details for provisioning the control plane for a cluster.

infrastructureRef.description: Reference to a ROSACluster object that holds the details for provisioning the infrastructure for a cluster.

paused.description: Paused can be used to prevent controllers from processing the Cluster and all its associated objects.

topology.description: Topology encapsulates the information for the cluster topology.

class.description: The name of the ClusterClass object to create the topology.

version.description: The Kubernetes version of the cluster.

status.description: ClusterStatus defines the observed state of a Cluster.

controlPlaneReady.description: ControlPlaneReady defines if the control plane is ready.

infrastructureReady.description: InfrastructureReady is the state of the infrastructure provider.

phase.description: Phase represents the current phase of cluster actuation.
